---
title: Composting basics
date: 2024-04-19
tags: [gardening, soil, compost]
---

A compost pile wants roughly three parts carbon to one part nitrogen by volume. I think of it as browns and greens: dry leaves, straw, cardboard, and sawdust on one side, and kitchen scraps, coffee grounds, and fresh grass clippings on the other.

Heat is the sign that things are going well. A well-built pile climbs to sixty degrees Celsius within a few days and stays warm for a week or two. If it stalls, it is usually too dry or too compacted, and a turn with a fork plus a bucket of water gets it moving again.

I keep two bins so one can rest while the other is active. Finished compost smells like forest floor and crumbles between the fingers. I screen it through hardware cloth and spread an inch on the vegetable beds each spring, which is the single best thing I do for the garden.
